scriptId: cs_v3_ACCUMULATION_L40_Renovating or fixing up the home
historyId: h_v3_ACCUMULATION_L40_Renovating or fixing up the home

goal: Obtain general renovation guidance for a home renovation.
Incorporate basic accessibility improvements incrementally to improve safety and livability for an elderly relative.
constraints: Avoid trendy choices that quickly date the house.
Design should be timeless and attractive to buyers.
Budget cap: approximately $18,000 for the entire renovation.
Prioritize function, safety, and budget efficiency; avoid major structural changes.
Maintain timeless design and durability; avoid trendy finishes.

Residents will live in the home during renovation; sequencing must preserve livability, maintain at least one functional kitchen and one bathroom, and implement enhanced dust containment and safety planning.
Incorporate basic accessibility improvements incrementally within budget; avoid full-home accessibility remodel.
decisions: Adopt phased renovation approach prioritizing structural and plumbing work first, followed by rough-ins and finish trades.
Determine initial accessibility upgrades to implement in Phase 1 (thresholds, grab bars, lever hardware, non-slip surfaces, accessible shower).
entities: Large-format porcelain tile (stone or concrete look)
High-quality hardware in a single cohesive finish (brass-look PVD or satin nickel)
isolate and cap branch lines for demo areas
indoor air quality management during demo
open: Decision needed: choose which room to tackle first (kitchen or bathroom).
Decision needed: sequencing under livable-renovation constraints; which room to tackle first and how to set up a temporary kitchen.
Decision needed: which accessibility features to implement in the first phase (entry, bathroom, doorway widths, etc.).
